figures.caption: Fig. 1: Numerical feature with a linear change of mean. We show a scatter plot of a sample of it (top) and a box plot of its ranking by each method over 20 runs (bottom).
Fig. 2: Numerical feature with an abrupt change of mean. We show a scatter plot of a sample of it (top) and a box plot of its ranking by each method over 20 runs (bottom).
Fig. 3: Numerical feature with a linear change of variance. We show a scatter plot of a sample of it (top) and a box plot of its ranking by each method over 20 runs (bottom).
Fig. 4: Numerical feature with an abrupt change of variance. We show a scatter plot of a sample of it (top) and a box plot of its ranking by each method over 20 runs (bottom).

Fig. 5: Categorical feature with a change in the relative frequency of classes. We show a line plot of their relative frequency each week (top) and a box plot of its ranking by each method over 20 runs (bottom).

Fig. 6: Categorical feature with a change in the domain. We show a line plot of the relative frequency of its classes each week (top) and a box plot of its ranking by each method over 20 runs (bottom).
Fig. 7: Numerical feature with erratic behaviour. We show a scatter plot of a sample of it (top) and a box plot of its ranking by each method over 20 runs (bottom).
Fig. 8: Rankings of injected features for multivariate change detection experiment.
Fig. 9: Scalability test on number of features (top) and instances (bottom) for each method. Notice the logarithmic scale on both axis.
